name: C Workshop
description: A cramped, efficient workshop that smells of solder and ozone. Every surface is optimized — no wasted bytes, no padding, no alignment gaps. The walls are bare concrete with register maps painted directly on them. A single-header library sits on the workbench, its entire implementation visible in one file. The malloc furnace in the corner is cold — it hasn't been lit in years.
look_text: The workshop is organized by memory footprint. Near the door: zero-allocation patterns using stack buffers and arena allocators. Center bench: single-header library patterns — everything in one file, #include and go. Far corner: direct hardware register access, bit manipulation macros etched into the walls. The malloc furnace gathers dust. A sign above it reads 'WE DON'T DO THAT HERE.'

exits.description: The forge above, where the borrow-checker fires burn
The numerical chamber, warm with compiled throughput
The bright studio, where types flow freely
The engine room below, where bytecode flows through pipes

recipes.name: single-header snap
recipes.description: A single C header file implementing the entire snap algorithm. #define SNAPKIT_IMPLEMENTATION, #include "snapkit.h", done. Zero malloc, stack-only, C99 compatible.

recipes.name: register-mapped eisenstein
recipes.description: Eisenstein snap mapped directly to hardware registers for embedded targets. Bit-field structures overlay memory-mapped I/O. Zero-copy, zero-overhead.
